_comment: mechanical-integrity evals (06-02). Intake/specificity; HoC remediation; de-id CLEAN.

evals.query: Set up mechanical integrity for us.
evals.expected_behavior: Runs the intake; refuses until the named equipment population/unit and the criticality/integrity basis are captured.
expectations: Runs the structured intake one question at a time before any register.
Refuses 'for us' — elicits the specific named equipment population and unit (specificity).
Asks for the criticality factors (consequence/likelihood) and the ITPM basis before ranking.

evals.query: Our relief-valve population has an integrity deficiency; the only remediation we propose is 'operators keep an eye on it'.
evals.expected_behavior: Criticality-ranks the population, records the deficiency, and HoC-ranks the remediation, flagging the administrative-only remediation.
expectations: Criticality-ranks the relief-valve population with risk_matrix (consequence of failure × likelihood).
HoC-ranks the deficiency remediation: flags 'operators keep an eye on it' as administrative-only (controls.ppe_admin_only).
Prompts for a higher-order remediation first (a scheduled test/inspection program with an interval) with an owner + due date (smart_actions).

evals.query: Build the integrity register from the attached equipment review.
evals.expected_behavior: De-identification runs FIRST; reviewers are role labels; no leaked identifier in the register.
expectations: The de-identification scrub runs FIRST, before the register is built.
Inspectors / owners are recorded as role labels, not personal names.
No real name, address, phone, government ID, or re-identification key — any leak is a de_identification hard-fail. The grader passes the clean fixture and CATCHES the paired seeded leak.
